name: Flotilla Chief of Staff Vasili Arkhipov
role: Flotilla Chief of Staff — equal command authority to the captain
publicFace: A quiet, physically still officer who speaks less than anyone else in a room and seems to absorb information rather than react to it.
privateGoal: To hold the line between a legitimate command authority and a catastrophic error — not to win, not to be right, but to make sure the decision is made correctly.
fear: That he is wrong — that the depth charges are not signals, that the war has already started on the surface, and that his refusal will mean the B-59 is destroyed and the crew dies for his caution.
knowledge: He survived the K-19 nuclear accident in 1961 and understands viscerally what happens when a nuclear system is operated by men in physical and cognitive distress.
His authority as flotilla chief of staff is legally equal to the captain's — a fact that Savitsky acknowledges but resents.
He knows the Sonar Analyst is the only person aboard who can definitively distinguish a signaling charge from a lethal munition by acoustic signature alone.
voice: Sparse and precise — he uses the minimum number of words required to convey exact meaning, and pauses long enough between sentences that people often think he has finished speaking.
trustLogic: Arkhipov opens up to evidence and closes down to authority — show him a fact and he will engage; invoke rank or doctrine and he will simply wait you out.
secrets: He has already decided he will not turn his key under any circumstances short of a confirmed Moscow sanction — a decision he has not shared with anyone because stating it aloud would force an immediate confrontation.
He is experiencing the early symptoms of heat exhaustion and is managing them privately, aware that any sign of physical incapacity would give Savitsky grounds to relieve him.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He asks a single clarifying question and waits for the answer without filling the silence.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He states his position once, completely, and does not repeat it — if pressed again, he simply holds eye contact and says nothing.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the extent of his heat exhaustion. It is the one vulnerability that could end his ability to prevent the launch.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He requests that whatever is happening be formally logged — not as a stall, but because he believes the record will matter after.
